notes: contains-mode-label fails 100% of baseline runs — the mode-dispatch framework is entirely a skill-added behavior, not emergent from Claude's base knowledge
contains-bote-pool-math: baseline passes on Pattern Guidance (4/5) but fails on Incident Analysis — BotE is inconsistently applied without the skill's mandatory BotE protocol
Eval 1 (pattern-guidance-bote) has the highest baseline score (4/5) — baseline Claude naturally addresses both options and provides decision rules, but lacks the structured mode label
Eval 2 (incident-analysis-pool-exhaustion) and Eval 3 (system-design-review) show the largest skill delta — the 9-step incident protocol and 7-dimension scorecard are high-value structural additions
KOS capture (mentions-kos-capture) is a pure skill differentiator — baseline never mentions it
Skill adds ~60s and ~14K tokens per eval — cost of the structured reasoning, BotE tables, and KOS integration
Baseline gives good advice but lacks: mode labels, BotE arithmetic, structured scorecards, KOS integration, and career-roadmap.md offers
